2670	\begin{lemma}
2671	[Surjectivity on sections from cofinal {\v C}ech-$H^1$ vanishing]
2672	  \label{lem:ses_cech_h1}
2673	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.ses_cech_h1}
2674	  \uses{def:cech_complex}
2675	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-ses-cech-h1}
2676	  % (read from references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1592--1628).
2677	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a ringed space.
2678	  % Let
2679	  % $$
2680	  % 0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{H} \to 0
2681	  % $$
2682	  % be a short exact sequence of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules.
2683	  % Let $U \subset X$ be an open subset.
2684	  % If there exists a cofinal system of open coverings $\mathcal{U}$
2685	  % of $U$ such that $\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0$,
2686	  % then the map $\mathcal{G}(U) \to \mathcal{H}(U)$ is
2687	  % surjective."
2688	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology,
2689	  \texttt{lemma-ses-cech-h1}.}
2690	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and let
2691	  \[
2692	    0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{H} \to 0
2693	  \]
2694	  be a short exact sequence of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. Let \(U \subset X\) be
2695	  open. If there is a cofinal system of open coverings \(\mathcal{U}\) of \(U\) with
2696	  \(\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\), then the map on sections
2697	  \(\mathcal{G}(U) \to \mathcal{H}(U)\) is surjective. Equivalently, the short exact
2698	  sequence is exact on sections over such \(U\).
2699	\end{lemma}
2700	
2701	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Take an element $s \in \mathcal{H}(U)$. Choose an open
2702	% covering
2703	% $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i$ such that
2704	% (a) $\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0$ and (b)
2705	% $s|_{U_i}$ is the image of a section $s_i \in \mathcal{G}(U_i)$.
2706	% Since we can certainly find $\mathcal{U}$ such that (b) holds
2707	% it follows from the assumptions of the lemma that we can find
2708	% $\mathcal{U}$ such that (a) and (b) both hold.
2709	% Consider the sections
2710	% $$
2711	% s_{i_0i_1} = s_{i_1}|_{U_{i_0i_1}} - s_{i_0}|_{U_{i_0i_1}}.
2712	% $$
2713	% Since $s_i$ lifts $s$ we see that $s_{i_0i_1} \in \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0i_1})$.
2714	% By the vanishing of $\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$ we can
2715	% find sections $t_i \in \mathcal{F}(U_i)$ such that
2716	% $$
2717	% s_{i_0i_1} = t_{i_1}|_{U_{i_0i_1}} - t_{i_0}|_{U_{i_0i_1}}.
2718	% $$
2719	% Then clearly the sections $s_i - t_i$ satisfy the sheaf condition
2720	% and glue to a section of $\mathcal{G}$ over $U$ which maps to $s$.
2721	% Hence we win."
2722	\begin{proof}
2723	  \uses{def:cech_complex}
2724	  Let \(s \in \mathcal{H}(U)\). Choose an open covering \(\mathcal{U} : U =
2725	  \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) fine enough that (a) \(\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U},
2726	  \mathcal{F}) = 0\) and (b) each restriction \(s|_{U_i}\) lifts to a section
2727	  \(s_i \in \mathcal{G}(U_i)\); both can be arranged simultaneously because the
2728	  coverings with property (a) are cofinal and (b) holds on any sufficiently fine
2729	  cover (surjectivity of \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{H}\) is a local condition).
2730	  On the double overlaps form
2731	  \[
2732	    s_{i_0 i_1} = s_{i_1}|_{U_{i_0 i_1}} - s_{i_0}|_{U_{i_0 i_1}}.
2733	  \]
2734	  Because the \(s_i\) all lift the single section \(s\), the difference
2735	  \(s_{i_0 i_1}\) maps to \(0\) in \(\mathcal{H}\), hence lies in the subsheaf
2736	  \(\mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 i_1})\); the family \((s_{i_0 i_1})\) is a {\v C}ech
2737	  \(1\)-cocycle for \(\mathcal{F}\). By \(\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\)
2738	  it is a coboundary: there are \(t_i \in \mathcal{F}(U_i)\) with
2739	  \(s_{i_0 i_1} = t_{i_1}|_{U_{i_0 i_1}} - t_{i_0}|_{U_{i_0 i_1}}\). Then the
2740	  corrected sections \(s_i - t_i \in \mathcal{G}(U_i)\) agree on overlaps, so by the
2741	  sheaf condition they glue to a global section of \(\mathcal{G}\) over \(U\) whose
2742	  image in \(\mathcal{H}\) is \(s\). Hence \(\mathcal{G}(U) \to \mathcal{H}(U)\) is
2743	  surjective.
2744	\end{proof}
2745	
2746	\begin{lemma}
2747	[Serre vanishing on affines]
2748	  \label{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}
2749	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.affine_serre_vanishing}
2750	  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis}
2751	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KG, Lemma
2752	  % \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} (Serre vanishing on
2753	  % affines) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L145--155).
2754	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Serre vanishing: Higher cohomology vanishes on affine schemes
2755	  % for quasi-coherent modules.
2756	  % Let $X$ be a scheme.
2757	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
2758	  % For any affine open $U \subset X$ we have
2759	  % $H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0$ for all $p > 0$."
2760	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KG
2761	  (\texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero}, Serre vanishing on
2762	  affines).}
2763	  Let \(U\) be an affine scheme and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
2764	  \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module. Then the sheaf cohomology of \(\mathcal{F}\) vanishes in
2765	  all positive degrees:
2766	  \[
2767	    H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0 \qquad \text{for all } p > 0.
2768	  \]
2769	\end{lemma}
2770	
2771	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "We are going to apply
2772	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-cech-vanish-basis}.
2773	% As our basis $\mathcal{B}$ for the topology of $X$ we are going to use
2774	% the affine opens of $X$.
2775	% As our set $\text{Cov}$ of open coverings we are going to use the standard
2776	% open coverings of affine opens of $X$.
2777	% Next we check that conditions (1), (2) and (3) of
2778	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-cech-vanish-basis}
2779	% hold. Note that the intersection of standard opens in an affine is
2780	% another standard open. Hence property (1) holds.
2781	% The coverings form a cofinal system of open coverings of any element
2782	% of $\mathcal{B}$, see
2783	% Schemes, Lemma \ref{schemes-lemma-standard-open}.
2784	% Hence (2) holds.
2785	% Finally, condition (3) of the lemma follows from
2786	% Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}."
2787	\begin{proof}

288	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — section {\v C}ech complex -/
289	
290	/-- **Cosimplicial abelian group underlying the section {\v C}ech complex.**
291	
292	For a family of opens `U : ι → Opens X` (an open covering) and a presheaf of modules `F`,
293	the cosimplicial object in `Ab` whose value in cosimplicial degree `p` is the product
294	`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` of sections over the `(p+1)`-fold intersections. A
295	cosimplicial map `f : [m] ⟶ [n]` acts at multi-index `σ : Fin (n+1) → ι` by projecting to
296	the coordinate `σ ∘ f` and then restricting along `⨅ₖ U (σ k) ≤ ⨅ₗ U (σ (f l))`. This is the
297	covariant ({\v C}ech) variance that the alternating-coface-map complex turns into a cochain
298	complex.
299	
300	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules. Built
301	as an honest `SimplexCategory ⥤ Ab` functor so that `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` supplies
302	`d² = 0` for free (no hand-rolled alternating-sum identity). -/
303	noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicial {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
304	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
305	  obj n := ∏ᶜ (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
306	  map {m n} f := Pi.lift (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
307	    Pi.π _ (σ ∘ f.toOrderHom) ≫
308	      F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ (f.toOrderHom l)))).op)
309	  map_id n := by
310	    apply Pi.hom_ext
311	    intro σ
312	    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, SimplexCategory.id_toOrderHom,
313	      OrderHom.id_coe, Function.comp_id]
314	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE _).op (𝟙 _)]
315	    simp
316	  map_comp {l m n} f g := by
317	    apply Pi.hom_ext
318	    intro σ
319	    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom, OrderHom.comp_coe]
320	    rw [← Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
321	    congr 1
322	
323	/-- **Section {\v C}ech cochain complex of a presheaf of modules**
324	(blueprint `def:section_cech_complex`).
325	
326	The cochain complex `Č•(𝒰, F)` of abelian groups with degree-`p` term
327	`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` and the alternating-sum-of-restrictions differential,
328	obtained as the alternating-coface-map complex of `sectionCechCosimplicial`. This is the
329	section complex of the blueprint, kept distinct from the relative pushforward `CechComplex`;
330	it is the right-hand side of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
331	`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)`.
332	
333	Project-local: no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules exists in Mathlib. -/
334	noncomputable def sectionCechComplex {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
335	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ :=
336	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).obj (sectionCechCosimplicial U F)
337	
338	end AlgebraicGeometry

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechAcyclic
open CategoryTheory Limits AlgebraicGeometry AlgebraicTopology

universe u
variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

-- general converse extraction
theorem sectionCech_objD_exact_of_isZero_homology {ι : Type u}
    (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (q : ℕ)
    (h : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology (q + 1))) :
    Function.Exact
      (ConcreteCategory.hom (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q))
      (ConcreteCategory.hom
        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1))) := by
  have hf : (sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)
      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q :=
    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) q
  have hg : (sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2)
      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1) :=
    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) (q + 1)
  have key : Function.Exact
      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)))
      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2))) := by
    have hh := h
    rw [← HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology,
        (sectionCechComplex U F).exactAt_iff' q (q + 1) (q + 2) (by simp) (by simp),
        ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact] at hh
    exact hh
  rw [hf, hg] at key
  exact key

-- coordinate cocycle -> coboundary
example {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules)
    (h : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology 1))
    (c : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
    (hcoc : ∀ σ : Fin 3 → ι,
      ∑ i : Fin 3, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
        ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i)
          (c (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) = 0) :
    ∃ t : ∀ σ : Fin 1 → ι, F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))),
      ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, c σ = ∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
        ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i)
          (t (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) := by
  have he := sectionCech_objD_exact_of_isZero_homology U F 0 h
  -- abstract cochain
  set c' : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk 1)) :=
    (sectionCechProductEquiv U F 1).symm c with hc'
  have hpe1 : sectionCechProductEquiv U F 1 c' = c := by rw [hc']; simp
  -- objD1 c' = 0
  have hzero : ConcreteCategory.hom
      (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) 1) c' = 0 := by
    apply (sectionCechProductEquiv U F 2).injective
    funext σ
    rw [sectionCech_objD_apply U F 1 c' σ, hpe1]
    rw [show sectionCechProductEquiv U F 2 0 σ = 0 from by
      rw [sectionCechProductEquiv_apply]; simp]
    exact hcoc σ
  obtain ⟨b, hb⟩ := (he c').mp hzero
  refine ⟨sectionCechProductEquiv U F 0 b, fun σ => ?_⟩
  rw [← hpe1, ← hb, sectionCech_objD_apply U F 0 b σ]

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Čech `H¹` vanishing ⟹ cocycles are coboundaries

The surjectivity-on-sections step `ses_cech_h1` (blueprint `lem:ses_cech_h1`, Stacks
`lemma-ses-cech-h1`) consumes the {\v C}ech-algebra fact that a covering with
`Ȟ¹(𝒰, F) = 0` has *every* {\v C}ech `1`-cocycle equal to a coboundary. This is the
`\uses{def:cech_complex}` content of that lemma, isolated here as a self-contained
statement about the section {\v C}ech complex `sectionCechComplex` (no sheaf gluing or
local-surjectivity input). It is the converse direction of
`sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact` (CechAcyclic), packaged in section
coordinates via `sectionCechProductEquiv` / `sectionCech_objD_apply`.

Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules, so
neither the homology-to-exactness reduction nor its coordinate form exist there. -/

section CechH1Coboundary

open AlgebraicTopology

/-- **Homology vanishing ⟹ coface-differential exactness** (converse of
`sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact`). If the degree-`(q+1)` homology of the
section {\v C}ech complex vanishes, the underlying group homomorphisms of the two
consecutive coface differentials `objD q`, `objD (q+1)` form an exact sequence. Pure
homological algebra: the same `exactAt_iff_isZero_homology` / `exactAt_iff'` /
`ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact` chain, run in the extraction direction.

Project-local: extraction form of the `Ab`-side homology bridge of
`lem:section_cech_homology_exact`, needed by the `ses_cech_h1` coboundary step. -/
theorem sectionCech_objD_exact_of_isZero_homology {ι : Type u}
    (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (q : ℕ)
    (h : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology (q + 1))) :
    Function.Exact
      (ConcreteCategory.hom (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q))
      (ConcreteCategory.hom
        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1))) := by
  have hf : (sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)
      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q :=
    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) q
  have hg : (sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2)
      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1) :=
    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) (q + 1)
  have key : Function.Exact
      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)))
      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2))) := by
    have hh := h
    rw [← HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology,
        (sectionCechComplex U F).exactAt_iff' q (q + 1) (q + 2) (by simp) (by simp),
        ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact] at hh
    exact hh
  rw [hf, hg] at key
  exact key

/-- **Čech `1`-cocycles are coboundaries when `Ȟ¹(𝒰, F) = 0`** — the
`\uses{def:cech_complex}` heart of `ses_cech_h1`.

Working in the section coordinates of `sectionCechCosimplicial` (a family of sections
`c σ ∈ F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` indexed by double-indices `σ : Fin 2 → ι`), if the family
satisfies the {\v C}ech `1`-cocycle identity (the alternating sum of its restrictions
over each triple-index `σ : Fin 3 → ι` vanishes) and the degree-`1` homology of the
section {\v C}ech complex vanishes, then `c` is a coboundary: there is a `0`-cochain
`t` (sections `t σ ∈ F(U (σ 0))` over single-indices) with `c σ` the alternating sum
of the face restrictions of `t`, i.e. `c σ = t_{σ 1}|_{σ} - t_{σ 0}|_{σ}` once the
two-term sum is expanded. The restriction maps are `sectionCechFaceRestr`, exactly the
{\v C}ech coface restrictions appearing in `sectionCech_objD_apply`.

Project-local: the section-coordinate coboundary extraction for the section {\v C}ech
complex has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
theorem sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology {ι : Type u}
    (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules)
    (h : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology 1))
    (c : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
    (hcoc : ∀ σ : Fin 3 → ι,
      ∑ i : Fin 3, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
        ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i)
          (c (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) = 0) :
    ∃ t : ∀ σ : Fin 1 → ι, F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))),
      ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, c σ = ∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
        ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i)
          (t (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) := by
  have he := sectionCech_objD_exact_of_isZero_homology U F 0 h
  set c' : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk 1)) :=
    (sectionCechProductEquiv U F 1).symm c with hc'
  have hpe1 : sectionCechProductEquiv U F 1 c' = c :=
    (sectionCechProductEquiv U F 1).apply_symm_apply c
  have hzero : ConcreteCategory.hom
      (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) 1) c' = 0 := by
    apply (sectionCechProductEquiv U F 2).injective
    funext σ
    rw [sectionCech_objD_apply U F 1 c' σ, hpe1]
    rw [show sectionCechProductEquiv U F 2 0 σ = 0 from by
      rw [sectionCechProductEquiv_apply]; exact map_zero _]
    exact hcoc σ
  obtain ⟨b, hb⟩ := (he c').mp hzero
  refine ⟨sectionCechProductEquiv U F 0 b, fun σ => ?_⟩
  rw [← hpe1, ← hb, sectionCech_objD_apply U F 0 b σ]

end CechH1Coboundary

  injectivity + `sectionCech_objD_apply` (q=1) coordinate-wise = `hcoc`; zero coordinate via

## End-of-session handoff

**Write a `## Summary` section** before the verdicts: declarations added (count + names), declarations blocked (count + why), sorry count before → after across your file.

**Write a `## Needs blueprint entry` section.** This mode is the biggest source of blueprint debt: every non-private definition/lemma you add is, by construction, infrastructure with **no blueprint block yet** — a `lean_aux` node invisible to the dependency graph (`archon dag-query unmatched` will list it) until the planner/reviewer blueprints it. List each new non-private declaration (name, file, and the facts its proof relies on) so the 1-to-1 Lean ↔ blueprint correspondence the dag agent built is restored next iter. Closing 0 project sorries while adding 3 unreported helpers does not advance the project — it quietly re-introduces the isolation the DAG phase worked to eliminate. Reporting them is mandatory.

**Write a `## Why I stopped` section** — be brutally honest. Only axiom-clean Lean declarations count as progress. Comments, sketches, and log prose do NOT. If you wrote down approaches you didn't attempt, say so:

- `Real progress`: N axiom-clean declarations added — name each one with its line number.
- `Partial progress`: added some declarations, hit a specific blocker on the next — name the blocker precisely (not "it's complex", but the exact type or missing ingredient).
- `Approaches written but not attempted`: identified routes in comments or log but did not attempt them — name each. This is valid but weak; the planner will re-assign with a directive to attempt them.
- `Blocked — alternatives exhausted`: informal agent called (or key unavailable, documented), alternative routes tried, all failed — name what was tried, why each failed, and the exact type statement of the next needed ingredient.
- `Infrastructure already exists`: Mathlib has what was needed — cite the exact lemma name and namespace.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
151	
152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155	
156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
157	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
159

10	/-!
11	# Free-presheaf Čech complex (P3b bridge — free-complex side)
12	
13	This file is the free-presheaf-complex side of the P3b bridge.  The section side lives in
14	`PresheafCech.lean`; this file owns the two free-complex declarations:
15	
16	- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreePresheafComplex`  (`def:cech_free_presheaf_complex`)
17	  — the chain complex of free presheaves of `O_X`-modules whose degree-`p` term is
18	  `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj`
19	  `  (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`.
20	
21	- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`  (`lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso`)
22	  — the free complex is a quasi-isomorphism / free resolution of `O_𝒰`.
23	
24	The quasi-isomorphism is proved objectwise (`quasiIso_of_evaluation`): for each open `V`,
25	the evaluated augmented complex is the augmented combinatorial Čech complex of the full
26	simplex on `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}` with constant coefficients `O_X(V)`, which is
27	contractible when `I₁(V) ≠ ∅` (via the prepend-`i_fix` homotopy
28	`FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy`) and zero when `I₁(V) = ∅`.  This file owns the
29	combinatorial engine (`FreeCechEngine`) supplying that contracting homotopy and the
30	per-`V` sectionwise reductions building toward `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`.
31	-/
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	/-
40	Planner strategy (P3b free-complex side; see analogies/p3b-presheafcech.md +
41	blueprint §Presheaf-level Čech machinery):
42	
43	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
44	`cechFreePresheafComplex`
45	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
46	Goal: a `ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ` whose degree-`p` term is
47	  `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`
48	with the alternating-face differential.
49	
50	Recommended build path — use the simplicial route so that d²=0 comes for free:
51	1. Construct a `SimplicialObject X.PresheafOfModules` whose `n`-simplices are the
52	   above direct sums.
53	2. Apply `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex` to obtain the chain complex.
54	   This gives d²=0 automatically via `SimplicialObject.boundarySquareZero`.
55	
56	Key API:
57	- `PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj :`
58	  `  ((Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u) ⥤ PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj`
59	  — the free-presheaf-of-modules functor.  Use it as `(PresheafOfModules.free _).obj` and
60	  `(PresheafOfModules.free _).map`.
61	- `yoneda.obj V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u` — the representable presheaf of sets at `V`.
62	  Do NOT introduce a bespoke `j_!`; `free ∘ yoneda` is the correct substitute.
63	- `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex` — turns a simplicial abelian group (or
64	  simplicial object in an abelian category) into a chain complex; d²=0 is a theorem.
65	- Direct sums: `⨁` is `DirectSum`; in `PresheafOfModules` use
66	  `Limits.biproduct` / `DirectSum.lof` / `DirectSum.desc`.
67	
68	DEAD END — do NOT hand-roll the alternating-sum identity for d²=0.  Use the simplicial
69	path above.
70	
71	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
72	`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`
73	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
74	Goal: show that `cechFreePresheafComplex` → `O_𝒰[0]` is a quasi-isomorphism (i.e.,
75	the free complex is a free resolution of the structure sheaf restricted to the cover).
76	
77	Recommended build path — objectwise contracting homotopy:
78	1. Homology in `X.PresheafOfModules` is computed objectwise (colimits are objectwise).
79	   So it suffices to exhibit, for each open `V : Opens ↥X`, a contracting homotopy on
80	   the sectionwise complex `cechFreePresheafComplex(V)`.
81	2. Sectionwise at `V`, degree `p` is `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} R(V)` for those multi-indices
82	   `σ` with `V ⊆ ⨅ k, U (σ k)`, and `0` otherwise.
83	3. Fix any `i_fix : ι` with `V ⊆ U i_fix` (if none exists, the complex is `0`).
84	   The homotopy `h : K_p(V) → K_{p+1}(V)` maps the `σ`-summand to the `(i_fix, σ)`-summand
85	   (prepend `i_fix`).  Check `dh + hd = id` at each degree.
86	4. Package as `HomologicalComplex.Homotopy`, then use `HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso`.
87	
88	Key API:
89	- `HomologicalComplex.Homotopy` — `Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Homotopy`.
90	- `HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso` (or `Homotopy.toQuasiIso`) — homotopy equivalence ⟹ quasi-iso.
91	
92	DEAD END — do NOT route through `SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy`.  That
93	interface has a different index convention and is not directly applicable here.
94	-/
95	
96	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — free-presheaf Čech complex
97	
98	This section builds the free-presheaf Čech complex `cechFreePresheafComplex` of a finite
99	open cover `𝒰 : X.OpenCover` as the alternating-face-map chain complex of an explicit
100	simplicial object `cechFreeSimplicial`.  Everything is project-local: Mathlib has the
101	free-presheaf-of-modules functor, the Yoneda embedding and `alternatingFaceMapComplex`,
102	but not their assembly into the Čech free resolution of a cover.
103	
104	We require `[Finite 𝒰.I₀]` so that the index types `Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` of the degreewise
105	coproducts are finite (`HasCoproductsOfShape`); this matches the finiteness hypothesis of
106	the downstream protected theorem `cech_computes_higherDirectImage`. -/
107	
108	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
109	
110	/-- **Free presheaf of modules on a representable open.**
111	
112	The composite `Opens X --yoneda--> ((Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type) --free--> X.PresheafOfModules`.
113	For an open `V`, `freeYoneda.obj V` is the extension-by-zero free presheaf of modules
114	`(j_V)_! O_X|_V` of the blueprint: on sections over `W` it is the free `O_X(W)`-module on
115	`Hom(W, V)`, i.e. `O_X(W)` if `W ⊆ V` and `0` otherwise.
116	
117	Project-local because Mathlib provides `PresheafOfModules.free` and `yoneda` separately but
118	not this composite, which is the basic building block of the free Čech complex. -/
119	noncomputable def freeYoneda : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules :=
120	  yoneda ⋙ PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj
121	
122	/-- The open underlying the `i`-th member of an open cover, as `(𝒰.f i).opensRange`. -/
123	def coverOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (i : 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X := (𝒰.f i).opensRange
124	
125	/-- The intersection open `⨅ k, U (σ k)` indexed by a tuple `σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀`.
126	
127	For `κ = Fin (p+1)` this is the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{σ(0)…σ(p)}` indexing the
128	degree-`p` term of the Čech complex. -/
129	def coverInterOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ : Type} (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X :=
130	  ⨅ k, coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k)
131	
132	/-- Reindexing along `α : κ' → κ` enlarges the intersection open: precomposing a tuple with
133	`α` keeps a subset of the indices, so the intersection grows. This `≤` supplies the
134	representable index-dropping maps that define the Čech differential. -/
135	lemma coverInterOpen_comp_le (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ κ' : Type} (α : κ' → κ) (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) :
136	    coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α) :=
137	  le_iInf fun k => iInf_le _ (α k)
138	
139	/-- A coproduct injection transported along an equality of indices: `eqToHom` of the induced
140	object equality cancels the index change. A generic categorical helper used to discharge the
141	dependent-index bookkeeping in the simplicial identities of `cechFreeSimplicial`. -/
142	private lemma sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport {C : Type*} [Category C] {β : Type*} (B : β → C)
143	    [HasCoproduct B] {a b : β} (e : a = b) :
144	    eqToHom (congrArg B e) ≫ Limits.Sigma.ι B b = Limits.Sigma.ι B a := by
145	  subst e; simp
146	
147	/-- **The free Čech simplicial object of a finite open cover.**
148	
149	The simplicial object in `X.PresheafOfModules` whose degree-`p` term is the coproduct
150	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)` and whose simplicial maps
151	reindex the multi-index `σ ↦ σ ∘ α` (along the order map of `α : [p] ⟶ [q]`) together with
152	the representable index-dropping inclusion `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α)`.
153	
154	Taking its `alternatingFaceMapComplex` produces `cechFreePresheafComplex` with `d² = 0` for
155	free (the simplicial route avoids hand-rolling the alternating-sum identity).
156	
157	Project-local: this is the simplicial backbone of the free Čech resolution; it has no
158	Mathlib counterpart. -/
159	noncomputable def cechFreeSimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
160	    SimplicialObject X.PresheafOfModules where
161	  obj n := ∐ fun σ : Fin (n.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)
162	  map {n m} α := Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ =>
163	    freeYoneda.map (homOfLE (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 α.unop.toOrderHom σ)) ≫
164	      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun τ : Fin (m.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ))
165	        (σ ∘ α.unop.toOrderHom)
166	  map_id n := by
167	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
168	    simp only [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Category.comp_id]
169	    have e : σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop) = σ := by funext i; simp
170	    have eo : coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop))
171	        = coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ := by rw [e]
172	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE
173	        (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 (SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop) σ)) (eqToHom eo.symm),
174	      eqToHom_map]
175	    exact sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport
176	      (fun σ : Fin (n.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) e
177	  map_comp {n m k} f g := by
178	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
179	    simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
180	    have e : σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (f ≫ g).unop)
181	        = (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom f.unop))
182	            ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom g.unop) := by
183	      funext i; simp [unop_comp, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom]
184	    rw [← sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport
185	          (fun τ : Fin (k.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) e,
186	      show (eqToHom (congrArg (fun τ : Fin (k.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
187	              freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) e))
188	          = freeYoneda.map (eqToHom (congrArg (coverInterOpen 𝒰) e)) from (eqToHom_map _ _).symm]
189	    simp only [← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
190	    congr 2
191	
192	/-- **The free-presheaf Čech complex of a finite open cover** (`def:cech_free_presheaf_complex`).
193	
194	The chain complex `K(𝒰)_•` in `X.PresheafOfModules` whose degree-`p` term is
195	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`
196	`= ⨁_σ (PresheafOfModules.free _).obj (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`, with the
197	alternating-sum index-dropping differential. Built as the `alternatingFaceMapComplex` of
198	`cechFreeSimplicial`, so `d² = 0` holds by the simplicial identities.
199	
200	Project-local: the free Čech resolution of a cover is not in Mathlib. -/
201	noncomputable def cechFreePresheafComplex (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
202	    ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ :=
203	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex X.PresheafOfModules).obj (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰)
204	
205	/-- Degreewise unfolding of the free Čech complex: the degree-`p` term is the coproduct of
206	free presheaves over the `(p+1)`-fold multi-indices. Exposed (it holds by `rfl`) so that the
207	downstream hom-identification and quasi-isomorphism arguments can rewrite the degree-`p`
208	object without unfolding `alternatingFaceMapComplex` and `cechFreeSimplicial` by hand. -/
209	lemma cechFreePresheafComplex_X (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (p : ℕ) :
210	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X p
211	      = ∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) :=
212	  rfl
213	
214	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — augmentation and cover structure presheaf -/
215	
216	/-- **Augmentation of a representable free presheaf onto the structure presheaf.**
217	
218	The canonical map `freeYoneda V ⟶ unit` (= `O_X`) corresponding under the free–Yoneda hom
219	bijection to the unit section `1 ∈ O_X(V)`. Concretely it sends the free generator over a
220	`W ⊆ V` to `1 ∈ O_X(W)`. This is the per-summand component of the Čech augmentation
221	`K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_𝒰`.
222	
223	Project-local: Mathlib has neither `freeYoneda` nor its augmentation. -/
224	noncomputable def freeYonedaAug (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
225	    freeYoneda.obj V ⟶ PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
226	  (freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).symm
227	    (1 : (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)))
228	
229	/-- Value of the free–Yoneda hom bijection on the augmentation: it is the unit section `1`. -/
230	lemma freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
231	    freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj) (freeYonedaAug V)
232	      = (1 : (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V))) := by
233	  simp only [freeYonedaAug, Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
234	
235	/-- Value of the augmentation `freeYoneda V' ⟶ O_X` on a generator `freeMk g` (`g : V ⟶ V'`):
236	it is the unit section `1 ∈ O_X(V)` (the restriction of `1 ∈ O_X(V')`). -/
237	private lemma freeYonedaAug_app_freeMk {V V' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (g : V ⟶ V') :
238	    (ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYonedaAug V').app (Opposite.op V))) (ModuleCat.freeMk g)
239	      = (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)) := by
240	  have key := (freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply V' (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)
241	      (freeYonedaAug V')).symm.trans (freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug V')
242	  have hmap : (ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYoneda.obj V').map g.op))
243	      (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V')) = ModuleCat.freeMk g := by
244	    erw [PresheafOfModules.freeObj_map, ModuleCat.freeDesc_apply]
245	    change ModuleCat.freeMk ((ConcreteCategory.hom ((yoneda.obj V').map g.op)) (𝟙 V'))
246	      = ModuleCat.freeMk g
247	    congr 1
248	  have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply (freeYonedaAug V') g.op
249	    (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V'))
250	  rw [hmap] at hnat
251	  rw [hnat]
252	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map g.op))
253	    ((ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYonedaAug V').app (Opposite.op V'))) (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V'))) = 1
254	  exact (congrArg (ConcreteCategory.hom (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map g.op)) key).trans (map_one _)
255	
256	/-- **Naturality of the augmentation.** For `V ≤ V'` the restriction map of free presheaves
257	composed with the augmentation onto `O_X` equals the augmentation over the smaller open:
258	`freeYoneda.map (incl) ≫ freeYonedaAug V' = freeYonedaAug V`. This is the cocone condition
259	that turns the per-summand augmentations into a chain map `K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]`. -/
260	lemma freeYoneda_map_comp_aug {V V' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (h : V ≤ V') :
261	    freeYoneda.map (homOfLE h) ≫ freeYonedaAug V' = freeYonedaAug V := by
262	  apply (freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).injective
263	  rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, PresheafOfModules.comp_app]
264	  erw [ModuleCat.comp_apply, Functor.comp_map,
265	    PresheafOfModules.free_map_app, ModuleCat.free_map_apply, freeYonedaAug_app_freeMk]
266	
267	/-- **Degree-`0` augmentation of the free Čech complex.** The map
268	`K(𝒰)_0 = ∐_{σ : Fin 1 → I} freeYoneda(U_σ) ⟶ O_X` assembled from the per-summand
269	augmentations `freeYonedaAug`. Its image presheaf is the cover structure presheaf `O_𝒰`. -/
270	noncomputable def cechFreeAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
271	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X 0 ⟶ PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
272	  Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ : Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYonedaAug (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)
273	
274	/-- **The cover structure presheaf** `O_𝒰` (`def:cover_structure_presheaf`).
275	
276	The image (as a presheaf of `O_X`-modules) of the degree-`0` augmentation `K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_X`
277	(`cechFreeAug`). Concretely `O_𝒰(W) = O_X(W)` when `W` is contained in some cover member
278	`U_i`, and `0` otherwise. The free Čech complex `K(𝒰)_•` is a resolution of `O_𝒰`
279	(`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`).
280	
281	Project-local: the image presheaf of a cover's augmentation has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
282	noncomputable def coverStructurePresheaf (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
283	    X.PresheafOfModules :=
284	  Limits.image (cechFreeAug 𝒰)
285	
286	/-- Each face map `δ i : K(𝒰)_1 ⟶ K(𝒰)_0` composed with the augmentation is the same
287	multi-indexed augmentation `∐_{σ : Fin 2 → I} freeYoneda(U_σ) ⟶ O_X`, independent of `i`.
288	This is the cosimplicial-identity input to `d ≫ aug = 0`: the two faces become equal after
289	augmenting, by naturality of `freeYonedaAug`. -/
290	private lemma cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (i : Fin 2) :
291	    (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).δ i ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰
292	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun σ : Fin 2 → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYonedaAug (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) := by
293	  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
294	  intro σ
295	  simp only [SimplicialObject.δ, cechFreeSimplicial, cechFreeAug, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc,
296	    Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Category.assoc]
297	  exact freeYoneda_map_comp_aug (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 _ σ)
298	
299	/-- The differential `K(𝒰)_1 ⟶ K(𝒰)_0` composed with the augmentation vanishes: the
300	alternating face sum `δ_0 - δ_1` is killed because both faces agree after augmenting
301	(`cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug`). This is the cochain-map condition for the augmentation
302	`K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]`. -/
303	private lemma cechFree_d_comp_aug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
304	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d 1 0 ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰 = 0 := by
305	  have hd : (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d 1 0
306	      = AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) 0 :=
307	    AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex_obj_d (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) 0
308	  -- Prove the simplicial-level identity as a standalone `have` so its composition `≫` is
309	  -- elaborated fresh at the `cechFreeSimplicial.obj` types — matching
310	  -- `cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug`. Rewriting `hd` into the goal first would pin the composition
311	  -- at the `.X` types of the chain complex, which are only definitionally (not syntactically)
312	  -- equal and block the rewrites.
313	  have main : AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) 0
314	      ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰 = 0 := by
315	    rw [AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD, Fin.sum_univ_two,
316	      Preadditive.add_comp, Preadditive.zsmul_comp, Preadditive.zsmul_comp,
317	      cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug, cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug]
318	    simp only [Fin.isValue, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, pow_zero, pow_one, one_zsmul, neg_one_zsmul]
319	    abel
320	  rw [hd]; exact main
321	
322	/-- The differential `K(𝒰)_1 ⟶ K(𝒰)_0` composed with the map onto the image presheaf
323	`O_𝒰 = image(cechFreeAug)` vanishes. Obtained from `cechFree_d_comp_aug` by cancelling the
324	mono `image.ι`. This is the cochain-map condition for the augmentation
325	`K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]` (`cechFreeComplexAug`). -/
326	private lemma cechFree_d_comp_factorThruImage (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
327	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d 1 0 ≫ Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰) = 0 := by
328	  rw [← cancel_mono (Limits.image.ι (cechFreeAug 𝒰)), Category.assoc, Limits.image.fac,
329	    Limits.zero_comp]
330	  exact cechFree_d_comp_aug 𝒰
331	
332	/-- **The augmentation chain map** `K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]` (`def:cover_structure_presheaf`).
333	
334	The chain map from the free Čech complex to the cover structure presheaf concentrated in degree
335	`0`, whose degree-`0` component is the canonical map `K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_𝒰 = image(cechFreeAug)` onto the
336	image presheaf. The chain-map condition `d ≫ aug = 0` is `cechFree_d_comp_factorThruImage`. The
337	quasi-isomorphism claim `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` asserts this map is a quasi-isomorphism.
338	
339	Project-local: the augmented free Čech resolution of a cover is not in Mathlib. -/
340	noncomputable def cechFreeComplexAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
341	    cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰 ⟶
342	      (ChainComplex.single₀ X.PresheafOfModules).obj (coverStructurePresheaf 𝒰) :=
343	  ((cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).toSingle₀Equiv (coverStructurePresheaf 𝒰)).symm
344	    ⟨Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰), cechFree_d_comp_factorThruImage 𝒰⟩
345	
346	/-- The degree-`0` component of the augmentation chain map is the canonical map onto the image
347	presheaf `O_𝒰`. -/
348	lemma cechFreeComplexAug_f_zero (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
349	    (cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰).f 0 = Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰) := by
350	  rw [cechFreeComplexAug, ChainComplex.toSingle₀Equiv_symm_apply_f_zero]
351	
352	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — objectwise detection of quasi-isomorphisms
353	
354	Homology in `PresheafOfModules R` is computed objectwise: the evaluation functors
355	`PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V` are jointly conservative and preserve homology, so a
356	morphism of complexes of presheaves of modules is a quasi-isomorphism as soon as each of its
357	evaluations is.  These three lemmas package that reduction.  Mathlib has the single-functor
358	statement `HomologicalComplex.quasiIso_map_iff_of_preservesHomology` (which needs the functor to
359	reflect isomorphisms — false for a single evaluation), but not the joint-conservativity version
360	needed to reduce a quasi-isomorphism of presheaf-of-module complexes to its sectionwise checks. -/
361	
362	open HomologicalComplex in
363	/-- For a functor `F` preserving homology, if the induced map on the homology of the mapped
364	complexes is an isomorphism, then `F` applied to the homology map is an isomorphism.  This is the
365	naturality square of `ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso` read off at a fixed degree. -/
366	private lemma isIso_Fmap_homologyMap {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι} {C₁ C₂ : Type*}
367	    [Category C₁] [Category C₂] [Preadditive C₁] [Preadditive C₂]
368	    {K L : HomologicalComplex C₁ c} (φ : K ⟶ L) (F : C₁ ⥤ C₂) [F.Additive] [F.PreservesHomology]
369	    (i : ι) [K.HasHomology i] [L.HasHomology i]
370	    [((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).obj K).HasHomology i]
371	    [((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).obj L).HasHomology i]
372	    (hiso : IsIso (HomologicalComplex.homologyMap ((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ) i)) :
373	    IsIso (F.map (HomologicalComplex.homologyMap φ i)) := by
374	  have key := ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso_hom_naturality ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ) F
375	  haveI hmid : IsIso (ShortComplex.homologyMap
376	      (F.mapShortComplex.map ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ))) := hiso
377	  change IsIso (F.map (ShortComplex.homologyMap ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ)))
378	  haveI hcomp : IsIso (ShortComplex.homologyMap
379	      (F.mapShortComplex.map ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ)) ≫
380	      (((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).obj L).mapHomologyIso F).hom) := inferInstance
381	  haveI hcomp2 : IsIso ((((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).obj K).mapHomologyIso F).hom ≫
382	      F.map (ShortComplex.homologyMap ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ))) := key ▸ hcomp
383	  exact IsIso.of_isIso_comp_left
384	    (((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).obj K).mapHomologyIso F).hom _
385	
386	/-- **Joint conservativity of evaluations.** A morphism of presheaves of `R`-modules is an
387	isomorphism as soon as each of its evaluations `(evaluation R V).map g` is, since the underlying
388	presheaf-of-abelian-groups functor reflects isomorphisms and a morphism of presheaves is an
389	isomorphism iff it is so objectwise.  Project-local: the joint-conservativity packaging is not in
390	Mathlib. -/
391	private lemma isIso_of_evaluation {C : Type*} [Category C] (R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat)
392	    {M N : PresheafOfModules R} (g : M ⟶ N)
393	    (h : ∀ V, IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V).map g)) : IsIso g := by
394	  suffices hh : IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R).map g) from
395	    isIso_of_reflects_iso g (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R)
396	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
397	  intro V
398	  have hV := h V
399	  rw [PresheafOfModules.evaluation_map] at hV
400	  haveI : IsIso (g.app V) := hV
401	  have e : ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R).map g).app V
402	      = (forget₂ (ModuleCat (R.obj V)) Ab).map (g.app V) := rfl
403	  rw [e]
404	  exact Functor.map_isIso (forget₂ (ModuleCat (R.obj V)) Ab) (g.app V)
405	
406	/-- **Objectwise reduction of quasi-isomorphisms.** A morphism `φ` of chain complexes of
407	presheaves of `R`-modules is a quasi-isomorphism if, for every object `V`, its evaluation
408	`(evaluation R V).mapHomologicalComplex |>.map φ` (a morphism of complexes of `R(V)`-modules) is a
409	quasi-isomorphism.  This is the formal core of the sectionwise argument that the free Čech complex
410	resolves the cover structure presheaf: homology of presheaves of modules is computed objectwise.
411	
412	Project-local: combines `isIso_of_evaluation` (joint conservativity) with `isIso_Fmap_homologyMap`
413	(evaluation preserves homology). -/
414	lemma quasiIso_of_evaluation {C : Type*} [Category C] (R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat) {ι : Type*}
415	    {c : ComplexShape ι} {K L : HomologicalComplex (PresheafOfModules R) c} (φ : K ⟶ L)
416	    [∀ i, K.HasHomology i] [∀ i, L.HasHomology i]
417	    (h : ∀ V, QuasiIso (((PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V).mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ)) :
418	    QuasiIso φ := by
419	  rw [quasiIso_iff]
420	  intro i
421	  rw [quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap]
422	  apply isIso_of_evaluation R
423	  intro V
424	  rw [PresheafOfModules.evaluation_map]
425	  haveI hqV : QuasiIsoAt (((PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V).mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ) i :=
426	    (h V).quasiIsoAt i
427	  rw [quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap] at hqV
428	  exact isIso_Fmap_homologyMap φ (PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V) i hqV
429	
430	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — combinatorial contracting-homotopy engine
431	
432	The objectwise reduction `quasiIso_of_evaluation` turns `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` into a
433	per-open-`V` statement: the evaluation of the augmented free Čech complex at `V` is the
434	augmented combinatorial Čech complex of the full simplex on `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}` with
435	constant coefficients the ring `O_X(V)`.  This section ports the constant-coefficient
436	combinatorial contracting homotopy — the prepend-`i_fix` map and its `d ∘ h + h ∘ d = id`
437	identity — into this file as a self-contained algebraic engine.
438	
439	This is the free-side analogue of `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy` in `CechAcyclic.lean`
440	(those declarations are `private` there, hence unavailable here); the proofs are the same
441	alternating-sum cancellation, specialised to the constant coefficient module.  Here `M` is
442	the constant coefficient module (to be `O_X(V)` in the application) and `ι` the index type
443	(to be `I₁(V)`). -/
444	
445	namespace FreeCechEngine
446	
447	variable {ι : Type*} {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] {n : ℕ}
448	
449	/-- Alternating coface (Čech) differential with constant coefficients in `M`:
450	`(d t)(σ) = ∑ⱼ (-1)ʲ • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)`, the alternating sum of the index-dropping
451	maps.  Free-side port of `CombinatorialCech.combDifferential`. -/
452	def combDifferential (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M :=
453	  fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (n + 1), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)
454	
455	/-- The contracting homotopy: prepend the fixed index `r`.  `(h u)(τ) = u (Fin.cons r τ)`.
456	Free-side port of `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy`. -/
457	def combHomotopy (r : ι) (u : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) : (Fin n → ι) → M :=
458	  fun τ => u (Fin.cons r τ)
459	
460	@[simp] lemma combHomotopy_zero (r : ι) :
461	    combHomotopy (M := M) (n := n) r 0 = 0 := by
462	  funext τ; simp [combHomotopy]
463	
464	/-- Composing `Fin.cons r` with the `(k+1)`-th coface map is `Fin.cons r` of the `k`-th
465	coface map: the bookkeeping identity behind the homotopy computation. -/
466	lemma cons_comp_succAbove_succ (r : ι) (σ : Fin (n + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (n + 1)) :
467	    (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove
468	      = Fin.cons r (σ ∘ k.succAbove) := by
469	  funext l
470	  refine Fin.cases ?_ ?_ l
471	  · simp
472	  · intro i; simp [Fin.succ_succAbove_succ]
473	
474	/-- **Contracting-homotopy identity** `d ∘ h + h ∘ d = id` on the constant-coefficient
475	Čech complex (Stacks `lemma-homology-complex`, the `dh + hd = id` computation).  Free-side
476	port of `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy_spec`. -/
477	lemma combHomotopy_spec (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) :
478	    combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) + combHomotopy r (combDifferential t) = t := by
479	  funext σ
480	  simp only [combDifferential, combHomotopy, Pi.add_apply]
481	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_succ (f := fun j : Fin (n + 2) =>
482	    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ j.succAbove))]
483	  have h0 : (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (n + 2)).succAbove = σ := by
484	    funext i; simp
485	  rw [h0]
486	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_smul, Fin.val_succ]
487	  rw [add_left_comm, ← Finset.sum_add_distrib]
488	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero (fun x _ => by
489	    rw [cons_comp_succAbove_succ, pow_succ, mul_comm, neg_one_mul, neg_smul]; abel), add_zero]
490	
491	/-- Every cocycle is a coboundary in positive degree: if `d t = 0` then `t = d (h t)`. -/
492	lemma combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M)
493	    (ht : combDifferential t = 0) : combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) = t := by
494	  have h := combHomotopy_spec r t
495	  rw [ht, combHomotopy_zero, add_zero] at h
496	  exact h
497	
498	/-- Sign-cancellation behind `d² = 0`: under the index swap
499	`(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` the alternating sign flips. -/
500	lemma combSign_flip (j : Fin (n + 2)) (i : Fin (n + 1)) :
501	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ)) * ((-1) ^ (i : ℕ))
502	      = - (((-1 : ℤ) ^ ((j.succAbove i : Fin (n + 2)) : ℕ))
503	            * ((-1) ^ ((i.predAbove j : Fin (n + 1)) : ℕ))) := by
504	  rcases lt_or_ge (i.castSucc) j with h | h
505	  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h,
506	        Fin.val_castSucc, Fin.val_pred]
507	    have hpos : 0 < (j : ℕ) := lt_of_le_of_lt (Nat.zero_le _) (by exact_mod_cast h)
508	    obtain ⟨m, hm⟩ : ∃ m, (j : ℕ) = m + 1 := ⟨(j : ℕ) - 1, by omega⟩
509	    rw [hm]; simp only [Nat.add_sub_cancel, pow_succ]; ring
510	  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h,
511	        Fin.val_succ, Fin.coe_castPred]
512	    rw [pow_succ]; ring
513	
514	/-- **`d² = 0`** for the constant-coefficient alternating Čech complex, via the
515	sign-reversing involution `(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)`. -/
516	lemma combDifferential_comp (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) :
517	    combDifferential (combDifferential t) = 0 := by
518	  funext σ
519	  simp only [combDifferential, Pi.zero_apply, Finset.smul_sum, smul_smul]
520	  rw [← Fintype.sum_prod_type (f := fun p : Fin (n + 2) × Fin (n + 1) =>
521	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (p.1 : ℕ) * (-1) ^ (p.2 : ℕ)) • t ((σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) ∘ p.2.succAbove))]
522	  apply Finset.sum_involution (fun p _ => (p.1.succAbove p.2, p.2.predAbove p.1))
523	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
524	    have harg : (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove
525	        = (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove := by
526	      funext k
527	      simp only [Function.comp_apply]
528	      rw [Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove]
529	    simp only [harg]
530	    rw [← add_smul, combSign_flip j i]
531	    simp
532	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _ _
533	    simp only [ne_eq, Prod.mk.injEq, not_and]
534	    intro hj
535	    exact absurd hj (Fin.succAbove_ne j i)
536	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
537	    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq]
538	    exact ⟨Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove j i, Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove j i⟩
539	  · intro a _; exact Finset.mem_univ _

541	/-- **Positive-degree exactness** of the constant-coefficient Čech complex
542	(`Function.Exact` form).  Free-side port of `CombinatorialCech.combDifferential_exact`. -/
543	lemma combDifferential_exact (r : ι) (n : ℕ) :
544	    Function.Exact (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M))
545	      (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 3) → ι) → M)) := by
546	  intro x
547	  constructor
548	  · intro hx
549	    exact ⟨combHomotopy r x, combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle r x hx⟩
550	  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
551	    exact combDifferential_comp y
552	
553	end FreeCechEngine

555	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — sectionwise reduction of the free Čech complex -/
556	
557	/-- **Evaluation preserves the degreewise coproduct of the free Čech complex.**
558	
559	The objectwise-reduction `quasiIso_of_evaluation` requires understanding the evaluation of
560	`cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰` at an open `V`.  Since the degree-`p` term is the coproduct
561	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)` and the evaluation functor
562	preserves finite colimits (`PresheafOfModules.Finite.evaluation_preservesFiniteColimits`,
563	with `Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` finite as `𝒰.I₀` is finite), evaluating commutes with the
564	coproduct:
565	`(evaluation V).obj (K(𝒰)_p) ≅ ∐_σ (evaluation V).obj (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))`.
566	This is the degree-`p` entry point for the sectionwise description
567	(blueprint `lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise`); each evaluated summand
568	`(evaluation V).obj (freeYoneda.obj W) = (ModuleCat.free (O_X(V))).obj (V ⟶ W)` is the free
569	`O_X(V)`-module on the (subsingleton) hom-set `V ⟶ W`, i.e. `O_X(V)` if `V ≤ W` and `0`
570	otherwise.
571	
572	Project-local: the assembly of evaluation-preserves-coproduct with the free Čech complex
573	has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
574	noncomputable def cechFreeEval_X (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
575	    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ) (p : ℕ) :
576	    (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj V).obj ((cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X p)
577	      ≅ ∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
578	          (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj V).obj
579	            (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) := by
580	  haveI : Limits.PreservesColimitsOfShape (Discrete (Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀))
581	      (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj V) := by
582	    haveI := PresheafOfModules.Finite.evaluation_preservesFiniteColimits X.ringCatSheaf.obj V
583	    infer_instance
584	  exact Limits.PreservesCoproduct.iso _ _

586	/-- **Evaluating `freeYoneda W` at an open `V ⊄ W` gives the zero module.**
587	
588	`(evaluation V).obj (freeYoneda.obj W) = (ModuleCat.free (O_X(V))).obj (V ⟶ W)` is the free
589	`O_X(V)`-module on the hom-set `V ⟶ W`.  When `V ≰ W` that hom-set is empty, so the module
590	is `0`.  This is the per-summand input to the empty case of the sectionwise reduction (and
591	kills the non-`I₁` summands in the nonempty case).
592	
593	Project-local: a degreewise vanishing statement for the project's `freeYoneda`. -/
594	lemma freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le {W V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (h : ¬ V ≤ W) :
595	    Limits.IsZero ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
596	      (freeYoneda.obj W)) := by
597	  haveI : IsEmpty (V ⟶ W) := ⟨fun f => h (leOfHom f)⟩
598	  haveI : Subsingleton ↑((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj
599	      (Opposite.op V)).obj (freeYoneda.obj W)) :=
600	    ⟨fun a b => Finsupp.ext (fun x => (IsEmpty.false x).elim)⟩
601	  exact ModuleCat.isZero_of_subsingleton _
602	
603	/-- **Evaluating `freeYoneda W` at an open `V ≤ W` gives `O_X(V)`.**
604	
605	When `V ≤ W` the hom-set `V ⟶ W` is a singleton (`Unique`), so the free `O_X(V)`-module on it
606	is `O_X(V)` itself.  This is the per-summand identification of the surviving (`I₁`) summands in
607	the sectionwise reduction (blueprint `lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise`): together with
608	`freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le` it gives the description
609	`K(𝒰)_p(V) = ⊕_{σ : V ≤ U_σ} O_X(V)`.
610	
611	Project-local: a degreewise identification for the project's `freeYoneda`. -/
612	noncomputable def freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le {W V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (h : V ≤ W) :
613	    (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj (freeYoneda.obj W)
614	      ≅ ModuleCat.of (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V))
615	          (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)) :=
616	  haveI : Unique (V ⟶ W) := ⟨⟨homOfLE h⟩, fun _ => Subsingleton.elim _ _⟩
617	  (Finsupp.LinearEquiv.finsuppUnique _ _ (V ⟶ W)).toModuleIso

619	/-- A coproduct of zero objects is a zero object: every coproduct injection out of a zero
620	object is zero, so the identity of the coproduct is zero. -/
621	lemma isZero_sigma_of_forall_isZero {C : Type*} [Category C] [Limits.HasZeroMorphisms C]
622	    {β : Type*} (f : β → C) [Limits.HasCoproduct f]
623	    (h : ∀ b, Limits.IsZero (f b)) : Limits.IsZero (∐ f) := by
624	  rw [Limits.IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero]
625	  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
626	  intro b
627	  rw [Category.comp_id, Limits.comp_zero]
628	  exact (h b).eq_zero_of_src _

630	/-- **Empty case of the sectionwise reduction (object level).**
631	
632	If no cover member `U_i` contains `V` (i.e. `I₁(V) = ∅`), then the degree-`p` term of the
633	free Čech complex evaluates to the zero module at `V`.  Indeed every multi-index intersection
634	`coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 (σ 0)`, so `V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ` would force `V` into a
635	cover member, contradiction; hence each summand vanishes by `freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le`
636	and the coproduct is zero.  This is the object-level input to
637	`cech_free_eval_empty`. -/
638	lemma cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
639	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) (hV : ∀ i, ¬ V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i) :
640	    Limits.IsZero ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
641	      ((cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X p)) := by
642	  refine Limits.IsZero.of_iso ?_ (cechFreeEval_X 𝒰 (Opposite.op V) p)
643	  apply isZero_sigma_of_forall_isZero
644	  intro σ
645	  apply freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le
646	  intro hle
647	  exact hV (σ 0) (le_trans hle (iInf_le (fun k => coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k)) 0))

654	epimorphism out of the zero module `(evaluation V).obj (K(𝒰)_0)`

656	lemma coverStructurePresheaf_eval_isZero_of_isEmpty (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]

658	    Limits.IsZero ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj

664	    Functor.map_epi (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V))

667	    ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).map
668	      (Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰)))
669	    (cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty 𝒰 V 0 hV)
